speaker: WORCESTER
text: O no, my nephew must not know, Sir Richard,

speaker: WORCESTER
text: The liberal and kind offer of the King.

speaker: VERNON
text: ’Twere best he did.

speaker: WORCESTER
text: Then are we all undone.

speaker: WORCESTER
text: It is not possible, it cannot be

speaker: WORCESTER
text: The King should keep his word in loving us.

speaker: WORCESTER
text: He will suspect us still and find a time

speaker: WORCESTER
text: To punish this offense in other faults.

speaker: WORCESTER
text: Suspicion all our lives shall be stuck full of eyes,

speaker: WORCESTER
text: For treason is but trusted like the fox,

speaker: WORCESTER
text: Who, never so tame, so cherished and locked up,

speaker: WORCESTER
text: Will have a wild trick of his ancestors.

speaker: WORCESTER
text: Look how we can, or sad or merrily,

speaker: WORCESTER
text: Interpretation will misquote our looks,

speaker: WORCESTER
text: And we shall feed like oxen at a stall,

speaker: WORCESTER
text: The better cherished still the nearer death.

speaker: WORCESTER
text: My nephew’s trespass may be well forgot;

speaker: WORCESTER
text: It hath the excuse of youth and heat of blood,

speaker: WORCESTER
text: And an adopted name of privilege—

speaker: WORCESTER
text: A harebrained Hotspur governed by a spleen.

speaker: WORCESTER
text: All his offenses live upon my head

speaker: WORCESTER
text: And on his father’s. We did train him on,

speaker: WORCESTER
text: And his corruption being ta’en from us,

speaker: WORCESTER
text: We as the spring of all shall pay for all.

speaker: WORCESTER
text: Therefore, good cousin, let not Harry know

speaker: WORCESTER
text: In any case the offer of the King.

speaker: VERNON
text: Deliver what you will; I’ll say ’tis so.

speaker: VERNON
text: Here comes your cousin.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: My uncle is returned.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Deliver up my Lord of Westmoreland.—

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Uncle, what news?

speaker: WORCESTER
text: The King will bid you battle presently.

speaker: DOUGLAS
text: Defy him by the Lord of Westmoreland.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Lord Douglas, go you and tell him so.

speaker: DOUGLAS
text: Marry, and shall, and very willingly.

speaker: WORCESTER
text: There is no seeming mercy in the King.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Did you beg any? God forbid!

speaker: WORCESTER
text: I told him gently of our grievances,

speaker: WORCESTER
text: Of his oath-breaking, which he mended thus

speaker: WORCESTER
text: By now forswearing that he is forsworn.

speaker: WORCESTER
text: He calls us “rebels,” “traitors,” and will scourge

speaker: WORCESTER
text: With haughty arms this hateful name in us.

speaker: DOUGLAS
text: Arm, gentlemen, to arms. For I have thrown

speaker: DOUGLAS
text: A brave defiance in King Henry’s teeth,

speaker: DOUGLAS
text: And Westmoreland, that was engaged, did bear it,

speaker: DOUGLAS
text: Which cannot choose but bring him quickly on.

speaker: WORCESTER
text: The Prince of Wales stepped forth before the King,

speaker: WORCESTER
text: And, nephew, challenged you to single fight.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: O, would the quarrel lay upon our heads,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: And that no man might draw short breath today

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: But I and Harry Monmouth! Tell me, tell me,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: How showed his tasking? Seemed it in contempt?

speaker: VERNON
text: No, by my soul. I never in my life

speaker: VERNON
text: Did hear a challenge urged more modestly,

speaker: VERNON
text: Unless a brother should a brother dare

speaker: VERNON
text: To gentle exercise and proof of arms.

speaker: VERNON
text: He gave you all the duties of a man,

speaker: VERNON
text: Trimmed up your praises with a princely tongue,

speaker: VERNON
text: Spoke your deservings like a chronicle,

speaker: VERNON
text: Making you ever better than his praise

speaker: VERNON
text: By still dispraising praise valued with you,

speaker: VERNON
text: And, which became him like a prince indeed,

speaker: VERNON
text: He made a blushing cital of himself,

speaker: VERNON
text: And chid his truant youth with such a grace

speaker: VERNON
text: As if he mastered there a double spirit

speaker: VERNON
text: Of teaching and of learning instantly.

speaker: VERNON
text: There did he pause, but let me tell the world:

speaker: VERNON
text: If he outlive the envy of this day,

speaker: VERNON
text: England did never owe so sweet a hope

speaker: VERNON
text: So much misconstrued in his wantonness.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Cousin, I think thou art enamorèd

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: On his follies. Never did I hear

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Of any prince so wild a liberty.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: But be he as he will, yet once ere night

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: I will embrace him with a soldier’s arm

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: That he shall shrink under my courtesy.—

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Arm, arm with speed, and, fellows, soldiers, friends,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Better consider what you have to do

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Than I that have not well the gift of tongue

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Can lift your blood up with persuasion.

speaker: MESSENGER
text: My lord, here are letters for you.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: I cannot read them now.—

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: O gentlemen, the time of life is short;

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: To spend that shortness basely were too long

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: If life did ride upon a dial’s point,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Still ending at the arrival of an hour.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: An if we live, we live to tread on kings;

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: If die, brave death, when princes die with us.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Now, for our consciences, the arms are fair

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: When the intent of bearing them is just.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MESSENGER
text: My lord, prepare. The King comes on apace.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: I thank him that he cuts me from my tale,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: For I profess not talking. Only this:

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Let each man do his best. And here draw I a sword,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Whose temper I intend to stain

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: With the best blood that I can meet withal

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: In the adventure of this perilous day.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Now, Esperance! Percy! And set on.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Sound all the lofty instruments of war,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: And by that music let us all embrace,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: For, heaven to Earth, some of us never shall

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: A second time do such a courtesy.
